2026-03-11T11:21:13.2461521Z **Key findings:**
2026-03-11T11:21:14.4088329Z - This is a regression in 4.9.0 caused by the default service version bump to `2025-07-01` (from `7.6` in 4.8.0)
2026-03-11T11:21:14.4103965Z - `EncodedPolicy` is only set during deserialization if the service response contains a `data` JSON property — if the new API version changed or omitted this field for mHSM keys, it stays `null`
2026-03-11T11:21:14.9518939Z - The suggested debugging path is to force `V7_6` on a 4.9.0 client to confirm whether the version bump is the trigger, then compare raw HTTP responses

2026-03-11T11:21:31.8230788Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-11T11:21:09.224276803Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** `KeyReleasePolicy.EncodedPolicy` is `null` after deserialization when fetching a key from Managed HSM using `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys` 4.9.0, causing a `NullReferenceException` on `.ToString()`. This is a regression from 4.5.0.\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Root Cause Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n### Most Likely Culprit: Default Service Version Bump in 4.9.0\n\nThe [4.9.0 changelog](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/CHANGELOG.md) states:\n\n\u003e The default service version is now `2025-07-01`.\n\nPrevious GA default was `7.6` (set in 4.8.0). This version bump is the most likely source of the regression.\n\n### How the deserialization works\n\nIn [`KeyReleasePolicy.cs`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/src/KeyReleasePolicy.cs), `EncodedPolicy` is only populated when the JSON response contains a `data` property:\n\n```csharp\ncase DataPropertyName:\n    byte[] data = Base64Url.Decode(prop.Value.GetString());\n    EncodedPolicy = new BinaryData(data);\n    break;\n```\n\nIf the service response omits `data` (or the field name changed in the `2025-07-01` API), `EncodedPolicy` remains `null` after deserialization — leading directly to the reported `NullReferenceException`.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🐛 Reproduction Steps\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n```csharp\nstring keyName = \"someKeyName\";\nstring? version = null;\n\n// Client targeting a Managed HSM endpoint\nKeyClient client = new KeyClient(new Uri(\"https://\u003cyour-mhsm\u003e.managedhsm.azure.net\"), new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\nKeyVaultKey keyVaultKey = await client.GetKeyAsync(keyName, version).ConfigureAwait(false);\n\n// ReleasePolicy is non-null (the check passes)\nKeyReleasePolicy hsmKeyReleasePolicy = keyVaultKey.Properties.ReleasePolicy;\nif (hsmKeyReleasePolicy == null)\n    throw new InvalidOperationException($\"Failed to get release policy for key=[REDACTED] EncodedPolicy is null — NullReferenceException thrown here\nhsmKeyReleasePolicy.EncodedPolicy.ToString();\n```\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🛠️ Debugging Strategies\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. **Confirm the service version is the trigger** — Force 4.9.0 to use the old service version:\n   ```csharp\n   var options = new KeyClientOptions(KeyClientOptions.ServiceVersion.V7_6);\n   KeyClient client = new KeyClient(vaultUri, credential, options);\n   ```\n   If this eliminates the `NullReferenceException`, the `2025-07-01` API is the culprit.\n\n2. **Inspect the raw HTTP response** — Enable logging to capture the JSON payload returned by the service and check whether the `data` field is present in the `release_policy` object.\n   ```csharp\n   var options = new KeyClientOptions { Diagnostics = { IsLoggingContentEnabled = true } };\n   ```\n\n3. **Compare JSON responses** — Check if `2025-07-01` changed the mHSM `release_policy` JSON field name (e.g., `data` → something else) or omits it in certain scenarios.\n\n4. **Check for null before calling `ToString()`** — As a short-term workaround:\n   ```csharp\n   var policyString = hsmKeyReleasePolicy.EncodedPolicy?.ToString() ?? \"\u003cnull policy data\u003e\";\n   ```\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Sub-tasks\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Reproduce the issue against the `2025-07-01` service version with an mHSM key that has a release policy set.\n- [ ] Compare the raw JSON response bodies for `7.6` vs `2025-07-01` for the same key.\n- [ ] If the API version changed the response shape, update `KeyReleasePolicy.ReadProperties` to handle the new field name/structure.\n- [ ] If `data` can legitimately be absent in the response, consider making `EncodedPolicy` null-safe or documenting this as a valid state.\n- [ ] Add a unit or live test covering deserialization of a `KeyReleasePolicy` with and without the `data` field.\n- [ ] Verify whether the same regression exists for non-mHSM Key Vault keys with release policies.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📚 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [`KeyReleasePolicy.cs`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/src/KeyReleasePolicy.cs) — Deserialization logic\n- [mHSM Release Policy Grammar](https://aka.ms/policygrammarmhsm)\n- [Azure Key Vault Release Policy Grammar](https://aka.ms/policygrammarkeys)\n- [4.9.0 Changelog](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/CHANGELOG.md)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\nCC: @Azure/azure-sdk-write-keyvault","item_number":56965},"name":"add_comment"}}}

2026-03-11T11:21:31.8759504Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-03-11T11:21:31.8759794Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-03-11T11:21:31.8759954Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-03-11T11:21:31.8760167Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-03-11T11:21:31.8760514Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-03-11T11:21:31.8760633Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
